mission.title: Install and Configure Parsec on Kubernetes
mission.description: PARSEC is an open-source initiative that provides a common API for hardware security and cryptographic services in a platform-agnostic way. Installing PARSEC allows you to manage keys and perform cryptographic operations without needing to know the underlying hardware details.

steps.title: Install Parsec from package
steps.description:
Install Parsec on Linux:
```bash
# Debian/Ubuntu
curl -Lo parsec.deb https://github.com/parallaxsecond/parsec/releases/download/1.4.1/parsec_1.4.1-1_amd64.deb
sudo dpkg -i parsec.deb

# Or build from source
cargo install parsec --version 1.4.1
```
See https://parallaxsecond.github.io/parsec-book/parsec_service/install_parsec_linux.html

steps.title: Configure Parsec
steps.description:
Create a configuration file:
```bash
sudo mkdir -p /etc/parsec
sudo cat > /etc/parsec/config.toml << 'EOF'
[core_settings]
log_level = "info"

[listener]
listener_type = "DomainSocket"
timeout = 200
socket_path = "/run/parsec/parsec.sock"

[[key_manager]]
name = "mbed-crypto-provider"
provider_type = "MbedCrypto"
key_info_manager = "sqlite-manager"
EOF
```

steps.title: Start the Parsec service
steps.description:
```bash
sudo systemctl enable parsec
sudo systemctl start parsec
```
Or run directly:
```bash
parsec -c /etc/parsec/config.toml &
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Install the Parsec CLI tool and test:
```bash
cargo install parsec-tool
parsec-tool ping
parsec-tool list-providers
```

resolution.summary: Parsec is installed as a platform-agnostic security API daemon for cryptographic key management.

uninstall.title: Stop and remove Parsec
uninstall.description:
```bash
sudo systemctl stop parsec
sudo systemctl disable parsec
sudo dpkg -r parsec
# Or: cargo uninstall parsec
```

upgrade.title: Download latest release
upgrade.description:
```bash
curl -Lo parsec.deb https://github.com/parallaxsecond/parsec/releases/latest/download/parsec_amd64.deb
sudo dpkg -i parsec.deb
sudo systemctl restart parsec
```

troubleshooting.title: Socket permission denied
troubleshooting.description:
Ensure the socket directory exists with correct permissions:
```bash
sudo mkdir -p /run/parsec
sudo chmod 755 /run/parsec
```

troubleshooting.title: Provider not available
troubleshooting.description:
Check supported hardware security modules:
```bash
parsec-tool list-providers
```
Fallback to the Mbed Crypto software provider.

prerequisites.description: Linux system required. Optionally requires hardware security modules (TPM, HSM). No Kubernetes cluster needed.
